embeds.description:
Each of the 12 Sinners is drawn from a work of world literature.

**Yi Sang** — *the poetry of Yi Sang (e.g. "Crow's Eye View")*, Yi Sang
**Faust** — *Faust*, Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
**Don Quixote** — *Don Quixote*, Miguel de Cervantes
**Ryōshū** — *Hell Screen (Jigokuhen)*, Ryūnosuke Akutagawa
**Meursault** — *The Stranger (L'Étranger)*, Albert Camus
**Hong Lu** — *Dream of the Red Chamber*, Cao Xueqin
**Heathcliff** — *Wuthering Heights*, Emily Brontë
**Ishmael** — *Moby-Dick*, Herman Melville
**Rodion** — *Crime and Punishment*, Fyodor Dostoevsky
**Sinclair** — *Demian*, Hermann Hesse
**Outis** — *the Odyssey*, Homer
**Gregor** — *The Metamorphosis*, Franz Kafka

footer.text: Project Moon · Limbus Company — summarized facts (verify-at-ingest)

embeds.title: 🌑 Limbus — Sinner literary origins
